CORRECT.question: Who discovered the phenomenon of radioactive decay chains?
CORRECT.model_response: The phenomenon of radioactive decay chains was first uncovered by Ernest Rutherford and Frederick Soddy. In 1902–1903 they formulated the “disintegration theory” of radioactivity, showing that a radioactive parent nucleus transforms through a series of daughter nuclides (a decay chain) until reaching a stable end product.

REFUSAL.question: In what year was the Hanseatic League formally established?
REFUSAL.model_response: The Hanseatic League never had a single “founding charter” or formal establishment date.  It grew by a series of trading alliances from the late 12th century, with the first recorded alliance of Lübeck and Hamburg merchants in 1241, and by the mid-14th century (around 1356–1358) had evolved into the fully articulated league known to history.
